title: Migrating EBS volumes from gp2 to gp3

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on Services the menu in the top, then click on Ec2 in the Compute section.
Navigate to Instances on the left panel and click on Launch Instances Button.
Name : Enter MyEC2Server
For Amazon Machine Image (AMI): Choose Amazon Linux 2023 kernel-6.1 AMI.
For Instance Type: select t2.micro
For Key pair: Select Create a new key pair Button
Key pair name: WhizKey
Key pair type: RSA
Private key file format: .pem
Select Create key pair Button.
In Network Settings Click on Edit Button:
Auto-assign public IP: Enable
Select Create new Security group
Security group name : Enter MyEC2Server_SG
Description : Enter Security Group to allow traffic to EC2.
In Configure storage, choose gp2.
Keep Rest thing Default and Click on Launch Instance Button.
Select View all Instances to View Instance you Created
Launch Status: Your instance is now launching, Click on the instance ID and wait for complete initialization of the instance till status changes to Running.
Note down the Instance ID of the EC2 instance. A sample is shown in the screenshot below

tasks.title: Task 3: Migrating the EBS Volumes from gp2 to gp3

steps.text: Make sure you are in the US East (N.Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on the Volumes under Elastic Block Store section on the left navigation panel.
In the Search Box, paste the Instance ID of your EC2 Instance.
Select the Volume associated with your EC2 Instance.
After selecting Volume, expand the Actions button and click on Modify Volume.
In the Modify Volume, expand Volume type.
Click on General Purpose SSD (gp3).
Keep Rest thing Default and Click on Modify Button.
You will get a prompt to Modify the Volume, Click on Modify Button.
It will take some time for the Volume to be modified.
After some time, the Volume State status will change to inuse and type will also be changed to gp3 from gp2.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Completion and Conclusion
You have successfully logged in to AWS Console.
You have successfully created an EC2 Instance.
You have successfully migrated volumes from gp2 to gp3.
You have successfully validated the lab.
End Lab
Sign out from the AWS Management Console.
Click on End Lab button from the lab console and wait till the process gets completed.
